passages.passage_text:
Directions (Q. Nos. 56-57)
Statements Some squares are circles.
No circle is a triangle.
No line is a square.
Give answer
(a) if only Conclusion I follows
(b) if only Conclusion II follows
(c) if either I or II follows
(d) if neither I nor II follows
(e) if both I and II follow

Directions (Q. Nos. 58-59)
Statements All songs are poems.
All poems are rhymes.
No rhyme is a paragraph.
Give answer
(a) if only Conclusion I follows
(b) if only Conclusion II follows
(c) if either I or II follows
(d) if neither I nor II follows
(e) if both I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All glasses are frames. Some frames are metals. All blocks are glasses.
Conclusions: I. All blocks are either metals or glasses. II. Some blocks are metals. III. Some farmers are blocks.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some dews are drops. All drops are stones.
Conclusions: I. Atleast some dews are stones. II. Atleast some stones are drops.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: I. All Philosophers are fools. II. All fools are illiterate.
Conclusions: I. All Philosophers are illiterate. II. All illiterates are Philosopher. III. All illiterates are fools. IV. Some illiterates would by Philosopher

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: Some beads are rings. Some rings are bangles. All bangles are clothes. All clothes are boxes.
Conclusions: I. Some boxes are bangles. II. Some clothes are rings. III. Some bangles are beads.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All chairs are fires. Some fires are winds. All winds are nets. Some nets are clocks.
Conclusions: I. Some clocks are winds. II. Some nets are fires. III. Some winds are chairs.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statements: All desks are pillars. All pillars are circles. Some circles are squares. Some squares are rectangles.
Conclusions: I. Some rectangles are pillars. II. Some circles are desks. III. Some squares are desks.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Conclusions: I. All squares can never be triangles. II. Some lines are circles.

choices.c: if either I or II follows
choices.d: if neither I nor II follows
choices.e: if both I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Conclusions: I. No triangle is a square. II. No line is a circle.

choices.c: if either I or II follows
choices.d: if neither I nor II follows
choices.e: if both I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Conclusions: I. No song is a paragraph. II. No poem is a paragraph.

choices.c: if either I or II follows
choices.d: if neither I nor II follows
choices.e: if both I and II follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Conclusions: I. All rhymes are poems. II. All songs are rhymes.

choices.c: if either I or II follows
choices.d: if neither I nor II follows
choices.e: if both I and II follow
